videos.title: Microsoft Build event in 25 minutes
videos.description: Microsoft just wrapped its annual Build conference for 2026. Along with agent-first Windows updates, we got a look at the Surface RTX Spark Dev Box, powered by Nvidia’s new Arm-based RTX Spark chips. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang then dialed in to talk about their collaboration on the new Dev Box and Surface Laptop Ultra. Plus, we got a look at the AI agent Project Solara, which is made to run AI agents on devices taking the form of everything from smart speakers to security badges and earbuds. Here’s

videos.title: Nvidia announces the RTX Spark
videos.description:
The RTX Spark is effectively the same GB10 chip that’s in the DGX Spark, the tiny “personal AI supercomputer” that Nvidia released last year, only now it’s a family of chips instead of just one. The Verge’s Sean Hollister explains today on The Vergecast.

videos.title: Nvidia just started a new chip war | The Vergecast
videos.description: Nvidia is betting that AI is going to change the way you use your computer — and with a new chip, the RTX Spark, it's hoping to ensure it powers that new-fangled AI machine. During a big week for the PC industry, with the Computex trade show and Microsoft's Build developer conference happening simultaneously, The Verge's Sean Hollister explains what's inside the Spark, why Nvidia is taking on Apple, Intel, AMD, and the rest of the chip industry, and whether the world's most valuable company has

videos.title: OpenClaw on Windows demo
videos.description:
Peter Steinberger, creator of OpenClaw, comes to Microsoft Build to talk through how OpenClaw will be integrated on Windows as a native Windows app paired with new security features called Microsoft Execution Containers. He says “You can totally run OpenClaw inside your company now” after the team does an onstage demo using a Surface Laptop Ultra.

videos.title: Microsoft announces “Project Solara"
videos.description:
Microsoft just announced “Project Solara,” a new OS designed for gadgets that run AI agents, at Build 2026. Microsoft demonstrated two concept Project Solara devices: Desk concept and badge concept. The desk concept is an Amazon Echo Show-like device that unlocks with facial recognition and provides access to AI agents. The badge concept is a wearable, the type of badge you’d typically use to access a work building. Link in bio for more from The Verge's senior news reporter Jay Peters.

Subscrib

videos.title: Microsoft's new Surface RTX Spark Dev Box
videos.description:
The new Surface RTX Spark Dev Box is powered by Nvidia’s new Arm-based RTX Spark chips, just like the Surface Laptop Ultra, and is optimized for sustained workloads and local AI tasks. This miniature Surface PC also has 128GB of unified memory, making it capable of running up to 120 billion parameter models locally. Link in bio for more from The Verge's senior correspondent Tom Warren.

videos.title: Casey Neistat's take on short-form
videos.description:
Casey Neistat breaks down the difference between “lean forward” and “lean back” content and why he’s never been interested in chasing short-form video trends.

videos.title: Casey Neistat's guide to posting every day | The Vergecast
videos.description:
The Vergecast is officially a daily show! We kick things off with the return of 90 Seconds on The Verge, a peek at the top stories on theverge.com. Then, we turn to our old pal Casey Neistat for some advice. Casey vlogged every day for 800 days straight, and has some thoughts on the pros and cons of daily posting, the state of YouTube in 2026, and how to make things every day without losing your mind in the process.

0:00 Intro - The Vergecast goes daily
1:10 90 Seconds on The Verge
2:44 Welcome
